tasks.title: Refactor quest objective completion detection into unified QuestCompletionEngine
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-completion-engine.ts as a centralized engine that evaluates all 19 canonical objective types against real game state. Currently quest completion checks are scattered across BabylonGame.ts, QuestObjectManager.ts, and route handlers. (1) Define a QuestCompletionEngine class with evaluateObjective(objective, gameState) → { completed: boolean, progress: number, evidence: string }. (2) Implement evaluators for each canonical type in shared/quest-objective-types.ts: visit_location checks player position vs locationRadius, talk_to_npc checks conversation log, use_vocabulary checks vocabulary tracker, collect_item checks inventory, etc. (3) Add a checkAllObjectives(quest, gameState) method that returns per-objective status plus overall quest completion (all objectives completed = quest completed). (4) Wire this into both server-side route handlers and export a lightweight client-compatible version for real-time UI updates. (5) Include unit tests in server/__tests__/quest-completion-engine.test.ts covering each objective type.

tasks.title: Implement quest checklist UI with per-objective completion indicators
tasks.description: Enhance client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts quest log panel (toggled via J key) to show a clear checklist for each active quest. (1) Each quest objective renders as a checkbox line item with description text. (2) Completed objectives show a green checkmark and strikethrough text. (3) In-progress objectives show a progress bar (e.g., '3/5 vocabulary words used'). (4) Incomplete objectives show an empty checkbox. (5) When all objectives are checked off, the quest title gets a gold border and 'COMPLETE - Return to [NPC]' label. (6) Add subtle animation when an objective transitions from incomplete to complete (checkmark slide-in). (7) Use Babylon.js GUI StackPanel with per-objective rows. (8) Quest checklist updates in real-time as the QuestCompletionEngine emits progress events via GameEventBus.

tasks.title: Add conversation analysis pipeline for quest trigger detection
tasks.description: Create server/services/conversation/quest-trigger-analyzer.ts that inspects each conversation turn for quest-relevant triggers. (1) After each player message, analyze the text for: vocabulary usage (check against quest targetWords), grammar pattern usage (check against quest grammar objectives), topic relevance (NPC-specific quest topics), and task completion phrases. (2) Use keyword matching + fuzzy matching (Levenshtein distance ≤ 2) for vocabulary detection in the target language. (3) Emit quest_progress events via the conversation metadata stream when triggers are detected. (4) Integrate into the HTTP bridge (server/services/conversation/http-bridge.ts) response pipeline so trigger analysis runs on every turn. (5) Return a QUEST_PROGRESS marker block in the response metadata: **QUEST_PROGRESS** ObjectiveId: X, Progress: N/M, Trigger: 'used word X' **END_QUEST_PROGRESS**. (6) Client-side ConversationClient.ts parses this block and updates QuestCompletionEngine.

tasks.title: Define new in-game action types for language learning interactions
tasks.description: Expand the game action system in shared/game-engine/types.ts and create seed actions in a new migration server/migrations/020-language-learning-actions.ts. Add these new action types: (1) 'examine_object' - player inspects a world object and sees its name in target language (key: X or click), (2) 'read_sign' - player reads text on signs, menus, books in target language, (3) 'write_response' - player composes written text in response to a prompt, (4) 'listen_and_repeat' - NPC says a phrase, player must repeat it via speech, (5) 'point_and_name' - player points at object and names it in target language, (6) 'ask_for_directions' - player asks NPC for directions in target language, (7) 'order_food' - player orders at a restaurant/market in target language, (8) 'haggle_price' - player negotiates price in target language, (9) 'introduce_self' - player introduces themselves to NPC, (10) 'describe_scene' - player describes what they see in target language. Add keyboard binding X for examine_object in KeyboardMap.ts.

tasks.title: Implement NPC-initiated conversation system
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCConversationInitiator.ts that allows NPCs to start conversations with the player. (1) NPCs with active quests for the player, or NPCs flagged as mentors/teachers, can initiate conversation when the player is within a configurable proximity radius (default 5 units). (2) Show a speech bubble above the NPC with a greeting in the target language + 'Press G to respond'. (3) If the player doesn't respond within 10 seconds, the NPC calls out again with a different phrase. (4) NPC initiation is governed by a cooldown (minimum 60 seconds between attempts per NPC) and a global cooldown (minimum 30 seconds between any NPC initiation) to avoid overwhelming the player. (5) Priority system: assessment NPCs > quest-giver NPCs > mentor NPCs > ambient NPCs. (6) When conversation starts via NPC initiation, the system prompt includes context that the NPC initiated and should guide the conversation toward their goal (quest offering, assessment, teaching moment). (7) Integrate with NPCAmbientConversationManager.ts to prevent ambient conversations from interrupting initiated ones.

tasks.title: Wire InteriorNPCManager into BuildingEntrySystem for business owner placement
tasks.description: Connect the existing but unused InteriorNPCManager.ts to BuildingEntrySystem.ts so that business owners and employees appear inside their businesses when the player enters. (1) In BuildingEntrySystem.enterBuilding(), after interior generation, call InteriorNPCManager.populateInterior() passing the buildingId, generated interior, and building metadata from BabylonGame.buildingData Map (which already stores ownerId, employees[], businessType, businessName). (2) InteriorNPCManager already has logic to find candidate NPCs (owner during business hours 7-20, employees, visitors with extroversion weighting) and position them at furniture roles (counter, desk, bar for owner/employees; tables for visitors). Enable this by passing the character data and NPC mesh references. (3) On building exit, call InteriorNPCManager.clearInterior() to restore NPC overworld positions. (4) Respect NPCScheduleSystem: if an NPC is marked isInsideBuilding=true for this building, they should appear inside; if they've walked away per schedule, they should not. (5) Wire the NPC greeting trigger so the business owner greets the player in the target language when they enter. (6) Ensure the conversation system (G key) works with interior NPCs the same as overworld NPCs.

tasks.title: Create adaptive quest generation based on player learning profile
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/quest-generator.ts to generate quests specifically tailored to the player's learning gaps. (1) Load the player's LanguageProgress from shared/language/progress.ts including vocabulary mastery levels, grammar pattern accuracy, and assessment dimension scores. (2) Identify weak areas: vocabulary categories with < 60% mastery, grammar patterns with < 50% accuracy, low-scoring assessment dimensions. (3) Generate quests that specifically target these weak areas - e.g., if 'food vocabulary' is weak, generate a market shopping quest; if grammar accuracy for past tense is low, generate a storytelling quest requiring past tense. (4) Add a new function generateAdaptiveQuest(worldId, playerId) that queries progress data and constructs a targeted quest prompt for Gemini. (5) Include a 'learningObjective' field on the quest schema indicating what skill this quest targets. (6) Balance: 60% quests target weaknesses, 20% reinforce strengths, 20% introduce new material.

tasks.title: Integrate NPC exam/quiz system with existing assessment engine
tasks.description: Create server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts that leverages the already-built assessment infrastructure (shared/assessment/assessment-types.ts, AssessmentEngine.ts, AssessmentModalUI.ts, assessment-scoring.ts endpoints) to let NPCs administer periodic exams. The core assessment engine already supports 4 phase types (reading, writing, listening, conversation) with EVAL block scoring, CEFR mapping, and dimension tracking. (1) Define NpcExam as a lightweight wrapper around AssessmentDefinition from shared/assessment/assessment-types.ts, selecting 1-2 phases from the periodic encounter template rather than the full 4-phase assessment. (2) Exam triggers: after every 5 quests completed, after 30 minutes of gameplay, or when player requests via teacher NPC interaction. (3) Reuse the existing POST /api/assessments/generate-content and POST /api/assessments/score-phase endpoints for content generation and scoring. (4) Reuse AssessmentModalUI.ts for reading/writing/listening exam display and AssessmentProgressUI.ts for progress tracking. (5) Store exam results alongside periodic assessment results using the existing AssessmentResult type. (6) Wire exam initiation into NPCConversationInitiator.ts so teacher/professor NPCs can proactively start exams. (7) Exam NPCs use the existing tier-adaptive system prompts from assessment-prompt-utils.ts.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: object recognition in business contexts
tasks.description: Add object recognition exam type to server/services/npc-exam-engine.ts, designed to work especially well inside businesses. (1) When the player is inside a business, the business owner or employee can quiz them on objects visible in the interior (generated by BuildingInteriorGenerator.ts — counters, furniture, goods). (2) NPC points to or presents objects and asks 'What is this called?' in the target language. (3) For each object, show a visual highlight (glowing outline via Babylon.js HighlightLayer) on the target object while the NPC asks the question. (4) Player must type or speak the name in the target language. (5) Scoring: exact match = full points, close match (Levenshtein ≤ 2) = partial points, wrong = 0. Uses the existing score-phase endpoint for evaluation. (6) After each answer, NPC provides the correct answer with pronunciation guidance. (7) Objects selected based on business type (bakery → bread/oven/flour, restaurant → plates/cups/menu, blacksmith → tools/anvil/metal). (8) Add an OBJECT_HIGHLIGHT marker block so the server can tell the client which object to highlight.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: writing and reading prompts using assessment phases
tasks.description: Wire the existing reading and writing assessment phases into the NPC exam system. The assessment engine already has full support for reading comprehension (passage + questions) and writing assessment (prompts with scoring) via shared/assessment/arrival-encounter.ts and departure-encounter.ts templates, plus the generate-content and score-phase server endpoints. (1) For reading exams: teacher NPC triggers a reading phase using AssessmentModalUI to display a passage and questions. Content is generated via POST /api/assessments/generate-content with phaseType='reading', scaled to player CEFR level. Scoring via POST /api/assessments/score-phase returns per-question and per-dimension scores. (2) For writing exams: teacher NPC triggers a writing phase. Content template specifies prompt type (letter, description, message) appropriate to the player's level. Player types response in AssessmentModalUI multi-line input. (3) Business-contextualized variants: reading a menu, reading a posted notice, writing a complaint letter, writing a shopping list. (4) Results feed into the same LanguageProgress.assessmentDimensions tracking as formal assessments.

tasks.title: Implement NPC exam type: listening comprehension using assessment phase
tasks.description: Wire the existing listening assessment phase into the NPC exam system. The assessment engine already supports listening comprehension with audio + questions via shared/assessment/arrival-encounter.ts (phase 3: Listening, 13 points). (1) NPC triggers a listening phase: server generates a passage via POST /api/assessments/generate-content with phaseType='listening'. (2) Passage is spoken via TTS (existing streaming TTS pipeline in server/services/tts-stt.ts) but NOT displayed as text. (3) Player can request one replay ('Can you repeat that?'). (4) After listening, comprehension questions are displayed via AssessmentModalUI. (5) CEFR scaling already handled by the tier-adaptive prompts: A1 = 1-2 short sentences spoken slowly, A2 = 3-4 sentences moderate pace, B1 = short monologue normal pace, B2 = complex monologue with idioms. (6) Scoring via existing score-phase endpoint. (7) Business-contextualized: listening to a shopkeeper describe products, a waiter recite specials, a guide give directions. (8) Add a 'listening mode' flag to BabylonChatPanel that hides NPC text and shows audio waveform visualization.

tasks.title: Bridge arrival assessment to quest system for onboarding integration
tasks.description: Bridge the existing Arrival assessment (shared/assessment/arrival-encounter.ts, already fully implemented with 4 phases: Reading 15pts, Writing 15pts, Listening 13pts, Conversation 10pts = 53 total) into the quest tracking system so it appears in the quest log. The AssessmentEngine, AssessmentModalUI, AssessmentProgressUI, OnboardingLauncher, and assessment-scoring endpoints are all complete. (1) Create server/services/assessment-quest-bridge.ts that wraps the arrival assessment in a Quest record: each of the 4 assessment phases becomes a quest objective (completed when its phase score is recorded). (2) Auto-assign this quest when OnboardingLauncher detects first-time play (existing detection logic). (3) Map assessment phase completion callbacks (AssessmentEngine.onPhaseCompleted) to quest objective updates via GameEventBus. (4) The quest appears in the quest log (J key) showing 'Arrival Assessment' with 4 checklist items matching the phases. (5) Quest completion triggers when all 4 phases finish and CEFR level is assigned (existing flow via cefr-mapping.ts). (6) The quest is non-skippable and non-abandonable. (7) This is purely a bridge layer — NO changes to the assessment engine itself.

tasks.title: Bridge departure assessment to quest system with improvement report
tasks.description: Bridge the existing Departure assessment (shared/assessment/departure-encounter.ts, fully implemented, mirrors arrival with intermediate difficulty, 4 phases, 53 points) into the quest tracking system. (1) Create a 'Departure Assessment' quest wrapper in server/services/assessment-quest-bridge.ts with the same 4-phase-as-objectives approach as the arrival bridge. (2) Triggered when the player indicates they want to finish (via game menu or after completing a threshold of quests). (3) On completion, compare departure AssessmentResult scores against arrival scores (both stored via the existing AssessmentResult type in assessment-types.ts) to compute improvement deltas per dimension. (4) Generate a 'Language Learning Report Card' as quest completion reward: before/after dimension scores, vocabulary learned count, grammar patterns mastered, total conversations, CEFR level change, recommended next steps. (5) Quest completion triggers the end-of-game summary screen. (6) Leverage existing periodic assessment data (shared/assessment/periodic-encounter.ts) to show mid-game progress points on the report.

tasks.title: Implement quest feasibility validator against available game actions
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-feasibility-validator.ts that ensures every generated quest can actually be completed given the current game mechanics. (1) Maintain a registry of completable action types: movement actions (player can walk to locations), conversation actions (player can talk to NPCs), inventory actions (player can pick up/deliver items), vocabulary actions (player can use words in conversation), examination actions (player can examine objects), business actions (player can enter businesses and interact with owners). (2) For each quest objective, verify: the target NPC exists and is reachable, the target location exists and is navigable, required items exist in the world or can be spawned, vocabulary words are in the target language dictionary, referenced businesses exist and have owners. (3) Reject or modify quests with infeasible objectives before presenting them to the player. (4) Add a feasibility score (0-1) to each quest indicating how achievable it is. (5) Integrate as a validation step in both quest-generator.ts and quest-assignment-engine.ts, running after generation but before assignment.

tasks.title: Add business-specific NPC conversation context for shopkeepers and service workers
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts to inject rich business context when the player is talking to a business owner or employee inside their establishment. (1) When building NPC context, detect if the NPC has an associated business (via character.currentOccupationId → occupation.businessId) and if the player is inside that business (via BuildingEntrySystem state). (2) Inject business-specific context into the system prompt: business name, business type, inventory/services offered, menu items (for restaurants), goods available (for shops), prices, business hours. (3) The NPC should respond in-character: a baker discusses bread and pastries, a blacksmith discusses tools and metal work, a tavern keeper discusses drinks and local gossip. (4) Include business-appropriate vocabulary hints in the system prompt so the NPC naturally introduces relevant vocabulary. (5) For restaurants: inject a menu with items in the target language. For shops: inject an inventory list. For services: inject a service description. (6) This context is generated during world creation and stored on the business record.

tasks.title: Implement NPC-guided conversation mode for quest objectives
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts to support 'guided conversation' mode when the player is working on a conversation-based quest objective. (1) When the player has an active quest with a complete_conversation or use_vocabulary objective involving the current NPC, inject guidance into the NPC's system prompt. (2) The NPC should steer the conversation toward the quest topic naturally (not abruptly). (3) If the player goes off-topic, the NPC gently redirects: 'That's interesting, but I was wondering about...' (4) The NPC should use scaffolding techniques: ask leading questions, provide sentence starters, offer word hints when the player struggles (detected by long pauses or off-target responses). (5) Add a 'conversationGoal' field to the quest objective that specifies what the NPC should guide toward. (6) Track conversation turns and if the player hasn't made progress in 5 turns, increase hint frequency.

tasks.title: Implement quest chain system for progressive learning
tasks.description: Create server/services/quest-chain-manager.ts for multi-part quest sequences that build on each other. (1) Define a QuestChain type with ordered quest references, unlock conditions, and a chain-level reward. (2) Each quest in a chain can specify prerequisiteQuestIds that must be completed before it unlocks. (3) Create starter chains: 'First Words' (5 quests progressing from greetings → self-introduction → asking questions → describing things → telling a story), 'Market Day' (3 quests: learn food vocabulary → visit market → complete a purchase), 'Town Explorer' (4 quests: learn direction words → follow directions → give directions → navigate independently). (4) Chain progress is visible in the quest log with a chain progress bar. (5) Completing a full chain grants bonus XP and a special achievement. (6) Add schema field 'chainId' and 'chainOrder' to quest schema in shared/schema.ts.

tasks.title: Add examine_object action with target-language labeling
tasks.description: Implement the 'examine_object' action in client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts. (1) When player presses X near a world object (item, building, environmental prop) or inside a business interior (furniture, goods, tools generated by BuildingInteriorGenerator.ts), display a floating label showing the object's name in the target language with pronunciation guide. (2) Use Babylon.js GUI Label positioned above the object. (3) Label shows: target language name (large), romanization/transliteration (medium), English translation (small, faded). (4) Label persists for 5 seconds then fades. (5) First examination of an object adds it to the player's vocabulary list via the existing vocabulary tracking in LanguageProgress. (6) Subsequent examinations show a 'Known ✓' badge. (7) Objects need a targetLanguageName field populated during world generation - add this to the item schema and populate it via server/generators/world-generator.ts using the settlement's primary language. (8) Fire a 'vocabulary_examined' event on GameEventBus for quest trigger detection. (9) Business interior objects are especially rich targets: bakery goods, restaurant dishes, blacksmith tools, etc.

tasks.title: Add read_sign action for environmental text interaction
tasks.description: Implement 'read_sign' action for interacting with text in the game world, including business signage. (1) Buildings, shops, and landmarks display signs with text in the target language. (2) When the player approaches a sign and presses X, show a close-up panel with the sign text in target language, word-by-word translation on hover, and full English translation toggle. (3) Signs include: shop names and descriptions, street signs, notice board postings, menu boards outside restaurants, directional signs, business hours signs, price lists posted outside shops. (4) Each sign interaction logs which words the player encountered and whether they used the translation toggle. (5) Sign content is generated during settlement creation using the target language via the existing tracery grammar system in server/services/tracery-service.ts. (6) Add a 'signs' array to settlement data with position, text, and translation. (7) Fire 'sign_read' event for quest tracking. (8) Create reusable Babylon.js GUI panel component for sign display.

tasks.title: Add point_and_name action for vocabulary practice
tasks.description: Implement 'point_and_name' action allowing players to point at objects and name them. (1) When holding the P key, show a crosshair/pointer cursor. (2) The crosshair highlights objects the player looks at (raycast from camera). (3) When the player releases P while targeting an object, open a small input prompt: 'What is this called in [target language]?' (4) Player types or speaks their answer. (5) Compare answer against the object's targetLanguageName using fuzzy matching (Levenshtein ≤ 2 for partial credit, exact match for full credit). (6) Show immediate feedback: green flash + chime for correct, gentle correction with the right answer for incorrect. (7) Award vocabulary XP for correct answers, reduced XP for close answers. (8) Track accuracy over time per object/word. (9) Fire 'object_named' event for quest objective tracking. (10) Add P key binding to KeyboardMap.ts. (11) Works inside business interiors: point at tools, food, furniture to name them.

tasks.title: Implement vocabulary-focused quest objectives with spaced repetition
tasks.description: Create a spaced repetition layer for vocabulary quest objectives in server/services/quest-generator.ts. (1) Track when each vocabulary word was last practiced and how many times it was correctly used (already partially in LanguageProgress.vocabulary). (2) Calculate review intervals using a simplified SM-2 algorithm: new words review after 1 quest, then 3, then 7, then 14 quests. (3) When generating vocabulary-focused quests, prioritize words due for review. (4) New quest objective concept: 'review_vocabulary' - player must correctly use N words from their review queue in conversations. (5) Map this to the existing 'use_vocabulary' canonical type but populate targetWords from the spaced repetition queue rather than random selection. (6) Track repetition state in LanguageProgress: add 'interval', 'easeFactor', 'nextReviewAt' fields per VocabularyEntry. (7) Generate review quests automatically when the player has 5+ words due for review.

tasks.title: Implement quest rewards tied to language skill progression
tasks.description: Enhance quest completion rewards in server/routes.ts POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/complete. (1) Beyond XP, quests now award specific language skill boosts: vocabulary quests grant vocabulary dimension points, grammar quests grant grammar dimension points, conversation quests grant fluency points, comprehension quests grant comprehension points. (2) Define reward scaling: quest difficulty × objective count × performance multiplier (based on how well objectives were completed, not just pass/fail). (3) Performance multiplier: completed on first try = 1.5x, used hints = 1.0x, required multiple attempts = 0.75x. (4) Award bonus for completing quests in target language immersion mode (>80% target language in conversations). (5) Update LanguageProgress.assessmentDimensions when rewards are applied. (6) Show a detailed reward breakdown in the quest completion UI. (7) Unlock new vocabulary categories or grammar patterns as rewards for chain completion.

tasks.title: Create daily/recurring quest system for consistent practice
tasks.description: Add a daily quest system in server/services/daily-quest-generator.ts. (1) Each game day (configurable, default = 20 minutes real time), generate 3 daily quests: one vocabulary practice, one conversation challenge, one exploration task. (2) Daily quests are simpler than main quests (1-2 objectives each) and designed for quick completion (5-10 minutes). (3) Completing all 3 daily quests grants a streak bonus (increasing XP multiplier for consecutive days). (4) Daily quests rotate through different vocabulary categories and grammar patterns to ensure broad coverage. (5) Track daily quest completion streak in LanguageProgress.streakDays. (6) Show daily quests in a dedicated section of the quest log with a countdown timer. (7) If the player misses a day, the streak resets but they don't lose progress. (8) Wire into the existing quest assignment endpoint or add a new GET /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/daily endpoint.

tasks.title: Implement quest hints system with scaffolded assistance
tasks.description: Add a hint system for when players are stuck on quest objectives. (1) Each quest objective has up to 3 hint levels: Level 1 = vague directional hint ('Try visiting the market area'), Level 2 = specific guidance ('Talk to the baker about bread'), Level 3 = explicit instruction ('Say "pan" to the baker when she asks what you want'). (2) Player requests hints via a 'Hint' button in the quest checklist UI (costs a small XP penalty, reduces performance multiplier). (3) Hints are generated at quest creation time by Gemini as part of the quest generation prompt. (4) For vocabulary objectives, Level 3 hints include the actual target words with pronunciation. (5) For location objectives, Level 2+ hints add a waypoint marker on the minimap. (6) Track hint usage per objective for performance scoring. (7) NPCs can also give contextual hints during conversation if they detect the player is struggling with a quest objective involving them.

tasks.title: Generate business inventory and menus in target language during world creation
tasks.description: Enhance server/generators/world-generator.ts to generate target-language inventory for each business during settlement creation. (1) For restaurants/taverns: generate a menu with 8-15 items (appetizers, mains, drinks, desserts) with names in target language, English descriptions, and prices. (2) For shops/markets: generate an inventory of 10-20 goods with target-language names, categories, and prices. (3) For service businesses (blacksmith, tailor, healer): generate a services list with target-language descriptions and prices. (4) Store inventory on the business record as a new 'inventory' field: array of { nameLocal: string, nameEnglish: string, category: string, price: number, description: string }. (5) Use Gemini to generate culturally appropriate items for the world's language and setting. (6) This data feeds into business NPC conversation context (task 15) and business-specific quest templates. (7) Include CEFR-appropriate vocabulary: simple common items available at A1, specialized items at B2.

tasks.title: Implement automatic quest completion detection and celebration
tasks.description: Wire the QuestCompletionEngine into the game loop for automatic detection when all objectives are met. (1) In client/src/components/3DGame/BabylonGame.ts, add a periodic check (every 2 seconds) that evaluates all active quest objectives via the QuestCompletionEngine. (2) When all objectives for a quest are completed, trigger a celebration sequence: confetti particle effect, level-up sound, quest completion banner ('Quest Complete: [Quest Title]'). (3) Show the reward breakdown overlay for 5 seconds. (4) If the quest has a turn-in NPC, mark the NPC's indicator as ✓ (green) and prompt 'Return to [NPC] to claim your reward'. (5) If no turn-in NPC, auto-complete immediately. (6) Fire 'quest_completed' event on GameEventBus. (7) Update the server immediately via POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/complete. (8) For quest chains, immediately unlock the next quest and show 'New Quest Available!' notification.

tasks.title: Add NPC memory of past quest interactions
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/conversation/context-manager.ts to include quest history in NPC conversation context. (1) When building the NPC context, include a summary of quests this NPC has given the player and their completion status. (2) NPCs reference past quests naturally: 'Last time you helped me find those herbs, remember?' (3) If the player failed or abandoned a quest from this NPC, the NPC can offer it again with encouragement: 'Would you like to try again? I can give you some hints this time.' (4) NPCs who have given the player successful quests have increased trust/friendship, reflected in conversation tone. (5) Store quest-NPC associations in the quest record (assignerNpcId field). (6) Limit context to last 5 quests per NPC to stay within token budget. (7) This creates a sense of persistent relationship between player and quest-giving NPCs.

tasks.title: Implement context-aware quest generation using world state
tasks.description: Enhance server/services/quest-generator.ts to use rich world context when generating quests. (1) Load the full settlement data: all NPCs with occupations, all locations with types, all items, current time of day, weather, active events, all businesses with their inventories. (2) Generate quests that reference actual game entities: 'Go to [real bakery name] and order [real menu item] from [real baker NPC name]'. (3) Ensure generated NPC names match actual NPCs via the existing charName() helper. (4) Ensure location references match actual settlement locations. (5) If a quest mentions an item, ensure that item exists in a business inventory or can be created. (6) Add seasonal/time-based quest variations: morning quests involve market/breakfast vocabulary, evening quests involve dinner/social vocabulary. (7) Include cultural context from the world's language and setting for culturally authentic quest content. (8) Business quests reference actual business inventory items and prices.

tasks.title: Add quest difficulty indicators and CEFR alignment
tasks.description: Enhance the quest UI to clearly communicate difficulty and language level requirements. (1) Each quest shows a CEFR badge (A1, A2, B1, B2) indicating the language level required. (2) Display difficulty stars (1-5) based on objective complexity and vocabulary difficulty. (3) Color-code quests in the quest log: green = at or below player level, yellow = slightly above, red = significantly above. (4) Add a 'Recommended' tag to quests matching the player's current CEFR level. (5) Show estimated time to complete (based on objective count and type). (6) In the quest assignment engine, filter quests by player's CEFR level ± 1 (can attempt slightly above or below). (7) Tooltip on difficulty indicator explains what language skills are needed. (8) Update QuestIndicatorManager.ts to color the NPC quest icon (!) based on quest difficulty relative to player level.

tasks.title: Implement grammar-focused quest objectives
tasks.description: Add grammar practice as a first-class quest objective concept. (1) Create grammar-focused quest templates in shared/language/quest-templates.ts: 'Past Tense Tales' (tell an NPC about yesterday using past tense), 'Question Master' (ask 5 questions to NPCs using correct question formation), 'Polite Requests' (make formal requests at 3 different shops). (2) Quest trigger analyzer detects grammar pattern usage by sending the player's text through the existing grammar feedback pipeline. (3) Track which grammar patterns the player used correctly vs incorrectly during quest conversations. (4) Map to existing canonical types: complete_conversation with a grammar focus flag, or use_vocabulary with grammar patterns as the target. (5) NPC provides inline grammar corrections during quest conversations without breaking immersion ('Ah, you mean [corrected form]? Yes, that's right!'). (6) Add grammar pattern tracking per quest objective.

tasks.title: Implement role-play scenario quests centered on business interactions
tasks.description: Create structured role-play quests in server/services/quest-generator.ts where the player must play a specific social role in business and service contexts. (1) Scenario types: 'At the Doctor/Healer' (describe symptoms to healer NPC inside their practice), 'Restaurant Patron' (enter restaurant, read menu, order meal from waiter NPC), 'Market Haggler' (enter market, negotiate prices with vendor NPC), 'New Customer' (visit a shop, browse inventory, ask about products), 'Apprentice' (visit blacksmith/tailor/baker, learn about the trade), 'Guest at the Inn' (check in, request a room, ask about amenities). (2) Each scenario has a goal, required vocabulary set, expected dialogue patterns, and success criteria. (3) The business NPC plays their role using the business context (inventory, menu, services from task 26). (4) Scenarios require the player to enter the business building and interact with the interior NPC. (5) CEFR-scaled: A1 has constrained vocabulary and short exchanges; B2 requires nuanced negotiation. (6) Add 'scenario_type' field to quest objectives.

tasks.title: Implement quest log with filtering and statistics
tasks.description: Overhaul the quest log panel (J key) in BabylonGame.ts. (1) Three tabs: Active Quests, Completed Quests, Quest Stats. (2) Active tab shows quest checklists with real-time progress (from task 2). (3) Completed tab shows historical quests with completion date, performance rating (stars), and language skills practiced. (4) Stats tab shows: total quests completed, completion rate, average performance, vocabulary words learned through quests, grammar patterns practiced, CEFR progress over time (line chart). (5) Filter by: quest type (vocabulary, grammar, conversation, exploration, business), difficulty level, NPC giver, completion status. (6) Sort by: most recent, difficulty, reward value. (7) Search by quest title or keyword. (8) Each completed quest expands to show detailed breakdown: objectives completed, time taken, hints used, vocabulary used, grammar accuracy.

tasks.title: Implement NPC mentor system for guided learning
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCMentorSystem.ts for a dedicated mentor NPC relationship. (1) When the player enters the world, they are assigned a mentor NPC (teacher, professor, or elder character). (2) The mentor NPC is always available at a known location (school, library, or town square). (3) Mentor provides: quest recommendations ('I think you should try talking to the baker next'), learning tips ('Remember, in [language], adjectives come after nouns'), progress check-ins ('You've learned 20 new words this session! Great work!'). (4) Mentor initiates conversation when the player returns to their location after completing quests. (5) Mentor can assign custom quests based on observed player weaknesses. (6) Mentor relationship deepens over time, unlocking more advanced teaching. (7) Add 'isMentor' flag to NPC and 'mentorNpcId' to player session. (8) Mentor conversations have a special system prompt emphasizing pedagogical approach.

tasks.title: Create business scavenger hunt quest type
tasks.description: Create a scavenger hunt quest template that sends players into multiple businesses to find and name items. (1) Quest gives the player a list of 5-10 items to find across different businesses (bread at the bakery, a hammer at the blacksmith, herbs at the healer). (2) Player must enter each business, find the item, and name it correctly in the target language using examine_object or point_and_name. (3) Business owners can provide clues: 'Yes, I have that here. Look near the counter.' (4) The quest checklist shows items as a visual grid with silhouettes that fill in when found and correctly named. (5) Items are drawn from the business inventory data (task 26) ensuring they actually exist. (6) CEFR scaling: A1 = common objects with picture hints, A2 = everyday items with text-only clues in target language, B1 = items described with riddle clues, B2 = idiomatic expressions tied to items. (7) Bonus objective: find all items within a time limit.

tasks.title: Implement conversation quality scoring for quest objectives
tasks.description: Create server/services/conversation-quality-scorer.ts to evaluate conversation quality during quest objectives. (1) For complete_conversation objectives, don't just check 'did they talk?' but 'how well did they talk?' (2) Scoring dimensions: vocabulary richness (unique words / total words), grammar accuracy (errors per sentence), target language ratio (% of words in target language vs English), conversation depth (multi-turn exchanges vs single-turn), topic relevance (stayed on quest topic). (3) Each dimension scored 0-100, combined into an overall quality score. (4) Quest objective completion thresholds: minimum 40% quality to pass, 70% for full credit, 90%+ for bonus. (5) Quality score affects quest rewards via performance multiplier. (6) Show quality breakdown in quest completion summary. (7) Store conversation quality data for learning analytics. (8) Use the existing EVAL marker block data when available, supplement with text analysis when not.

tasks.title: Implement quest notification and reminder system
tasks.description: Create client/src/components/3DGame/QuestNotificationSystem.ts for timely quest-related notifications. (1) Toast notifications (top-right corner, auto-dismiss after 5 seconds): 'New Quest Available!', 'Objective Complete!', 'Quest Updated', 'Daily Quests Reset'. (2) Proximity reminders: when player walks near a quest-relevant NPC or business, show subtle reminder ('Quest: Talk to [NPC] nearby' or 'Quest: Visit [Business Name]'). (3) Idle reminders: if the player hasn't made quest progress in 5 minutes, show a gentle nudge. (4) Achievement-style popups for milestones: first quest complete, 5 quests complete, first chain complete, first perfect score. (5) Configurable notification frequency in game settings. (6) Audio cues: subtle chime for objective progress, triumphant jingle for quest completion. (7) Integrate with GameEventBus for event-driven notification triggers.

tasks.title: Implement quest abandonment, failure, and retry mechanics
tasks.description: Add quest abandonment and retry handling. (1) Player can abandon any quest from the quest log (except hardcoded assessment quests) via an 'Abandon' button. (2) Abandoned quests move to a 'Failed/Abandoned' section with the option to retry. (3) Retry resets all objective progress but keeps hint state. (4) Time-limited quests (daily quests) auto-fail when the timer expires. (5) Conversation-based objectives can fail if the player ends the conversation prematurely (before quality threshold met). (6) Failed objectives show a red X instead of empty checkbox. (7) The quest-giving NPC acknowledges failure gracefully: 'No worries, you can try again when you're ready.' (8) Track attempt count per quest. (9) After 3 failed attempts, auto-reduce quest difficulty or offer simplified objectives. (10) Add PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/:questId/abandon endpoint.

tasks.title: Add shopping and economic vocabulary quests tied to real businesses
tasks.description: Create quest templates for shopping/economic interactions using actual settlement businesses and their inventories. (1) 'Market Shopping' quest: player given a shopping list of 3-5 items drawn from real business inventories (task 26), must enter the correct business and request items by name in target language from the interior NPC (task 6). (2) 'Price Negotiation' quest: player enters a shop, examines goods, and haggles with vendor NPC using number vocabulary and comparison phrases. (3) 'Restaurant Order' quest: player enters restaurant, reads the actual generated menu (in target language), orders a meal, asks about ingredients. (4) 'Comparison Shopping' quest: player visits 2-3 shops selling similar items, compares prices, reports the best deal to mentor NPC. (5) Each template uses the business inventory data so item names and prices are consistent. (6) Merchant NPCs respond appropriately to correct/incorrect vocabulary using business context. (7) Quest completion requires 'purchasing' items (deducting from player gold), adding to inventory.

tasks.title: Add direction-giving and navigation quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates for direction/navigation language practice. (1) 'Follow the Guide' quest: NPC gives verbal directions in target language ('Turn left at the bakery, walk straight to the fountain, the library is on your right'), player must follow them to reach the destination. (2) 'Guide the Stranger' quest: lost NPC asks for directions to a specific business, player must give correct directions in target language. (3) 'Treasure Map' quest: player receives written directions in target language and must decode them to find a location. (4) Direction vocabulary introduced progressively: A1 = left/right/straight, A2 = near/far/between/behind, B1 = complex instructions with landmarks, B2 = elaborate directions with alternatives. (5) Use the actual settlement street network from StreetNetworkLayout.ts for realistic navigation. (6) Map follow_directions and navigate_language canonical objective types to these templates.

tasks.title: Add storytelling and narrative quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates for narrative/storytelling practice. (1) 'Story Circle' quest: player joins a group of NPCs and must contribute to a collaborative story in the target language. (2) 'Local Legend' quest: NPC tells a folk tale, player must retell it to another NPC (tests listening comprehension + oral retelling). (3) 'My Adventure' quest: player must describe their in-game experiences to an NPC journalist/scribe using past tense and descriptive vocabulary. (4) 'Picture This' quest: player sees a scene and must describe it to an NPC who can't see it. (5) Storytelling quests emphasize: past tense, sequencing words (first, then, finally), descriptive adjectives, emotional vocabulary. (6) NPC audience reacts to story quality. (7) Performance scored on vocabulary range, grammar accuracy, narrative coherence.

tasks.title: Implement social relationship quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates focused on building NPC relationships through language use. (1) 'Make a Friend' quest: player must have 3 successful conversations with a specific NPC, progressing from introduction → shared interests → making plans. (2) 'Community Helper' quest: player assists multiple business NPCs with small tasks (deliver messages between shops, explain a product to a customer, help a shopkeeper take inventory). (3) 'Peacemaker' quest: two NPCs are in a disagreement, player must mediate using polite language. (4) 'Party Planner' quest: player invites NPCs from different businesses to an event, requiring invitation language and time/place vocabulary. (5) Social quests build on the existing relationship system (friendship, trust levels). (6) Completing social quests increases relationship scores, which unlocks deeper conversations from that NPC.

tasks.title: Implement cultural immersion quests
tasks.description: Create quest templates that teach cultural knowledge alongside language. (1) 'Festival Day' quest: player participates in a cultural celebration, learning associated vocabulary. (2) 'Recipe Quest' quest: player visits a restaurant or bakery NPC who teaches a traditional recipe — player must follow instructions in target language (imperative verb forms, cooking vocabulary, measurements). (3) 'Cultural Exchange' quest: player explains aspects of their own culture to an NPC. (4) 'Proverb Hunter' quest: player collects local proverbs from elder NPCs and must explain their meaning. (5) 'Traditional Craft' quest: player visits artisan businesses (blacksmith, tailor, potter) and NPC demonstrates a craft, player must describe the process. (6) Cultural quests introduce formal/informal registers, idiomatic expressions, culturally-specific vocabulary.

tasks.title: Create business tour quest chain for early-game settlement introduction
tasks.description: Create a quest chain that introduces the player to all businesses in the settlement, combining exploration with vocabulary building. (1) 'Welcome to Town' chain: mentor NPC gives the player a tour route visiting each major business type. (2) At each business, the player must: enter the building (E key), greet the business owner in target language, learn 3-5 business-specific vocabulary words (via examine_object on interior items), complete a simple interaction (order something, ask about services). (3) Quest objectives per business: visit_location (reach the business), talk_to_npc (greet owner), identify_object (name 3 items), complete_conversation (have a short exchange about what the business sells). (4) Chain order: bakery → market → restaurant → blacksmith → healer → tailor → inn. (5) Each business visit builds vocabulary for the next quest in the chain. (6) Completing the full chain unlocks business-specific quests and the 'Town Regular' achievement. (7) This chain naturally teaches the player building entry mechanics and interior NPC interaction.

tasks.title: Implement NPC schedule-aware quest assignment
tasks.description: Integrate quest assignment with the NPC schedule system in client/src/components/3DGame/NPCScheduleSystem.ts. (1) When generating quests that reference specific NPCs, check the NPC's schedule to ensure they'll be at the referenced location. Business owner NPCs are at their business during work hours (7-20) per the schedule system. (2) Quest descriptions include contextual time references: 'Visit Maria at the bakery in the morning'. (3) If a quest NPC is not at their expected location, show a hint: '[NPC] is usually at [business] during business hours.' (4) Time-of-day quest variants: morning quests (bakery, market), afternoon quests (workshops, services), evening quests (tavern, inn). (5) Quest feasibility validator checks NPC schedule compatibility. (6) Special quests for following an NPC's daily routine, learning time-of-day and routine vocabulary.
